description: Iran appoints Mohammed Barger Zalgada as new security chief following the death of Ali Larajani in US-Israeli strikes. Brisbane's new $3.8 billion Victoria Park stadium design has been unveiled, featuring 63,000 seats with shaded seating and giant digital screens, with construction beginning 1 June. title: Emergency fuel cap plan revealed amid crisis | 7NEWS
description: The Australian government has a contingency policy document that recommends capping fuel purchases at $40 per motorist (equivalent to 16 litres) during fuel emergencies. Approximately 160 petrol stations across Victoria have run out of fuel, with the state experiencing a 300-400 per cent surge in fuel demand. Treasurer Jim Chalmers has warned the economic impact of the fuel crisis and Middle East conflict could rival the COVID pandemic, while electric vehicle purchases at auction dealerships have surged in response. title: Oil prices swing on uncertain Iran talks | 7NEWS
description: Oil prices have fluctuated significantly in response to Donald Trump's statements about potential negotiations with Iran, dropping 14% to $96 per barrel before climbing back to $102 as Iran denied the existence of any talks. Intense fighting continues between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon, with Israeli strikes flattening buildings in the south and Hezbollah gunfire killing a woman in northern Israel. The United States is deploying 3,000 additional troops to the Middle East, supplementing two vessels of Marines already en route to the region. title: Pauline Hanson secures $100k pay rise as One Nation grows | 7NEWS
description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson will receive a $100,000 salary increase, bringing her annual pay to approximately $310,000, following her party's elevation to minor party status in federal parliament. The party achieved this status after gaining five or more members, boosted significantly by Barnaby Joyce's defection to One Nation. Hanson has formally requested Prime Minister Anthony Albanese approve additional staffing resources to match those allocated to the Greens party. title: Trump Organisation paid for Gold Coast mayor's Florida visit | 7NEWS
description: Gold Coast Mayor Tom Tate's February visit to Mar-a-Lago in Florida was funded by the Trump Organisation, including accommodation, meals featuring an expensive tomahawk steak, and airport transfers. Council documents confirm the expenses exceeded $500, refuting earlier criticism that ratepayers' money was used. The visit was to witness a deal between Eric Trump and a Gold Coast developer for a proposed 91-storey, $1 billion tower in Surfers Paradise, expected to go through council approval this year. title: Missile strike hits Tel Aviv apartment building
description: A ballistic missile has slipped through Israel's Iron Dome aerial defence system, striking a residential area in Tel Aviv. #israel #iran #middleeast #telaviv #worldnews

title: Iran appoints new security chief
description: Iran has installed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as the country’s new security chief.  Zolghadr is a loyalist of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, and an Islamic Revolutionary Guard veteran - he replaces Ali Larijani, who was killed in an Israeli strike last week. #iran #security #middleeast #news #worldnews

title: Government's secret $40 fuel plan uncovered
description: An uncovered crisis document has revealed the Australian government previously considered a $40 fuel cap and a 16L buying limit to conserve petrol.  The plan, from 2019, was laid out in a national fuel emergency response manual, obtained under a freedom of information (FOI) request by The Daily Telegraph.  The measures would reportedly come into effect only if the Governor-General declared a national liquid fuel emergency.  A declaration would follow an advisory from the energy minister.  The $40 limit equates to about 16L under current petrol prices. #petrol #fuel #oil #fuelshortage #bowser

title: Man charged over bank robberies 30 years ago
description: A wanted man has been extradited over two historic bank robberies almost 30 years ago where duffle bags of cash were taken in regional NSW.  The first incident was on October 28, 1996 when an unknown man allegedly entered a bank in Coonabarabran, in NSW’s Central West, armed with a shotgun and demanded cash from staff.  He allegedly took a female customer hostage to secure his escape, holding her until he reached his motorcycle a short distance away. #robber #thief #crime #investigation #police

title: NASA plans new base on the moon
description: NASA has scrapped its planned orbital space station, instead pivoting towards the moon's surface for a new base. #nasa #space #astronomy #moon

title: NASA scraps Gateway station for permanent Moon base | 7NEWS
description: NASA has cancelled its Lunar Gateway orbital space station project to redirect resources towards building a permanent base on the Moon's surface. Administrator Jared Isaacman announced the agency will invest approximately $20 billion over seven years to construct the lunar base in stages, working with commercial and international partners. The announcement precedes NASA's Artemis 2 mission launching next week, which will send astronauts around the Moon for the first time since 1972, while plans for a nuclear-powered spacecraft to Mars by late 2028 were also unveiled. title: Calls for federal government to impose tax on gas exports
description: The Albanese Government is under pressure to hit gas exporters with a giant new tax. Crossbenchers say without the tax we're giving away billions of dollars that could fund better services for Australians. #gas #fuel #auspol #fuelprice
